handoff_goal: Specify Settings > Providers import/export for shareable provider configuration bundles, including optional high-friction secret export, previewed import with conflict handling, and no active/default provider transfer.

current_project_roles.scope: Store the discussion and handoff artifacts.
current_project_roles.evidence_source: User confirmation and current working directory.
current_project_roles.notes: Prior Workflows discussions are not the target.

current_project_roles.scope: Desktop Settings > Providers UI, provider API/store/types, server provider REST API/service/types, provider persistence and managed settings sync.

target_project_roles.scope: Add provider import/export capability for the current app's Settings provider surface.
target_project_roles.evidence_source: User request plus live repository reads.
target_project_roles.notes: Sharing is provider-record focused, not local active state transfer.

target_paths_still_to_verify: Exact i18n keys and location for new Settings provider dialog text.
Whether import/export logic should be extracted from Settings.tsx into a provider-specific component.
implementation_target.cross_project_cognition_note: Current project cognition cannot prove another project's implementation facts; no other target project was requested.

source_evidence.claim: Settings defaults to the providers tab and renders ProviderSettings for provider CRUD/test/activation/delete.
Provider API wrapper has CRUD/test/settings methods but no provider import/export wrappers.
Provider store wraps CRUD/test/activation and refreshes connected sessions after provider updates.
SavedProvider contains credential-bearing apiKey plus preset, base URL, API format, model mappings, context windows, and notes.
Provider REST API has no import/export routes today.
Providers are persisted in the cc-jiangxia app config providers index; activation syncs provider-managed env into managed settings.
Existing helpers mask/restore provider-related secret env values for Settings JSON.
Existing import/export interaction precedent includes JSON export, preview, diagnostics, conflict handling, and commit.
Provider tests cover CRUD, activation sync, active deletion conflict, malformed settings recovery, auth strategies, and proxy behavior.
Cognition graph is unavailable because the database is missing.

soft.unknown: Exact UI placement, dialog layout, and warning copy.
soft.stop_and_reopen_condition: Spec cannot express import/export states or warnings clearly.

soft.unknown: Raw-vs-masked provider API key behavior conflict.
soft.stop_and_reopen_condition: Export security relies on an unverified frontend masking assumption.

settled_decisions: Provider import/export covers saved provider records only.
Active/default provider selection is excluded from export and import.
Secret-including export is a separate dangerous action with warning and second confirmation.
The app must not remember or preselect secret export.
Secret-bearing export artifacts must be clearly labeled.
Conflict default is add/rename; overwrite requires explicit selection.
Import does not auto-activate imported providers or write active-provider env.
Imported providers should get generated IDs by default.
Official login/OAuth state is not exported.
assumptions_to_preserve: Provider records may include sensitive credentials and must be redacted server-side for default export.
Workflow import/export is a UI/process precedent, not a provider schema source.
conflicts_requiring_return_to_discussion: A later requirement wants default export to include credentials.
A later requirement wants import to auto-activate providers.
A later requirement wants export/import to include active/default provider selection.
capability_map: Export with secrets through high-friction path.
Parse and validate provider bundle JSON.
Preview valid, invalid, conflicting, secret-bearing, and secret-missing entries.
Commit selected import candidates with add/rename/overwrite resolutions.
recommended_sequence: Define provider bundle schema and redaction semantics.
Define server-side export and import preview/commit contracts.
Add provider service import/export logic with validation and tests.
Add Settings > Providers UI controls and dialog.
Add focused desktop tests for interaction states and warnings.
reopen_conditions: Secret export becomes a remembered checkbox or unlabeled artifact.
Implementation relies on unresolved API key masking behavior.

confirmed_ui_decisions: Provider import/export lives in Settings > Providers.
Secret export must not be casual or remembered.
Import preview must show conflicts and credential status before commit.
deferred_ui_decisions: Whether export selected/all is one dialog or separate actions.
interaction_expectations: Use a preview step before import commit.
Display explicit warnings for secret-bearing exports and imports.
Avoid revealing secrets in routine diagnostics.
accessibility_expectations: Dialog controls must be keyboard reachable.
Warning and error states must be announced in text, not color alone.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Add Settings > Providers import/export so provider configurations can be shared with other people.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must center provider configuration sharing.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Import/export covers saved provider records only.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Bundle schema excludes unrelated app state.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Spec includes chat, MCP, skills, workflows, plugins, or global settings.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Users may choose whether to export API keys/secrets.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec includes both secret-free and secret-including export paths.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Secret export is omitted or made default.
Default export includes API keys or tokens.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Secret-including export requires a separate dangerous action, warning, second confirmation, no remembered choice, and clear artifact labeling.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Secret export is a remembered checkbox or unlabeled artifact.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Import conflicts default to add/rename; overwrite requires explicit selection.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec must include previewed conflict resolutions.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Import silently overwrites an existing provider.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Active/default provider selection is not exported or imported.
Import uses preview before commit with diagnostics for invalid entries, conflicts, missing secrets, and secret-bearing entries.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Import writes during preview or lacks diagnostics.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Existing workflow import/export dialog is a useful UI/process precedent, not a domain model to reuse blindly.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Spec may borrow interaction shape while defining provider-specific schema.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Workflow template semantics leak into provider bundle design.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Resolve raw-vs-masked provider API key behavior before claiming export security.
must_preserve_ledger.downstream_requirement: Plan or implementation design must verify and handle server-side redaction.
must_preserve_ledger.stop_and_reopen_condition: Export security relies only on frontend masking assumptions.

senior_consequence_analysis.trigger_reason: Provider import/export can expose credentials by explicit user choice, mutate provider persistence, affect active runtime settings if implemented incorrectly, collide with existing providers, and interact with managed settings preservation.
affected_object_map: Provider records in cc-jiangxia providers index.
Provider API responses and provider bundle artifacts.
Active provider ID, by exclusion from import/export.
cc-jiangxia managed settings env, by ensuring import does not activate or sync env.
Settings > Providers list, dialogs, and warning states.
Connected sessions that currently refresh on provider update.

state_behavior_matrix.behavior: Export empty/disabled state; import can add providers.
Provider record exportable, but active selection excluded.
Credentials redacted/omitted and artifact marked secret-free.
Requires separate action, warning, second confirmation, no remembered choice, and artifact marked credential-bearing.
Default add/rename, overwrite only by explicit selection.

dependency_impact_table.impact: Needs export, preview, and commit behavior without bypassing validation.
Import must not write active provider env.
Needs import/export actions and refresh after commit.
Needs controls, dialogs, warnings, preview, commit states.

recovery_and_validation_contract: Import commit is additive by default.
Overwrite is explicit and should re-read current provider state.
Import never changes active/default provider selection.
Failed import must not mutate managed settings env.
Tests prove default export redacts secrets and secret export requires explicit flag/path.

coverage_gaps.routing_decision: Continue with live evidence; carry gap.
coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Spec claims graph-proven ownership without live evidence.

coverage_gaps.gap: Provider API key masking behavior has conflicting evidence.
coverage_gaps.stop_and_reopen_condition: Export security relies only on frontend masking.

consequence_obligations.claim: Default export must not include credentials; optional secret export is explicit only.
Import preview must be side-effect free.
Import/export must not carry active/default provider selection or mutate active runtime env.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Import changes active provider or active env.

consequence_obligations.claim: Conflict handling defaults to add/rename; overwrite is explicit.
New imports use generated IDs by default.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Imported IDs collide or shadow without preview resolution.

consequence_obligations.claim: Resolve raw-vs-masked API key evidence conflict before claiming security.
Secret export uses high-friction protection and credential-bearing artifact labeling.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen_condition: Secret export is casual, remembered, or unlabeled.

checks: Context boundary is locked to the current repository.

Soft unknowns have owners, latest resolve phases, and stop/reopen conditions.
Must-Preserve Ledger includes goal, scope, non-goals, decisions, scenario, reference, and blocking evidence question.

Markdown and JSON companion were written together.
User confirmation is still required before marking handoff-ready.
